Oracc: CAMS/GKAB — CAMS Geography of Knowledge Corpus

STT 1, 041 [Gilgameš Letter][via cams/gkab]

Obverse
oo NaN  (start of obverse missing)
o 1'o 1'

[...] x x [GAL]-⸢MEŠ ša⸣ [...] ⸢KU₃.SI₂₂⸣ liq-⸢ti uḫ⸣-[ḫu-zu]

(o 1') I wrote to you [...] the [great ...] ... of [...] plated with liqtu-gold, to attach [...] Enkidu, my friend, and [...]: "There are none".

o 2'2'

[... den-ki]-du₃ ⸢ib⸣-ri-ia ⸢ša₂⸣ ra-⸢ka⸣-[si aš₂-pu]-⸢ra⸣-kam₂-ma ia-aʾ-⸢nu⸣ [...]

o 3'3'

[... al]-tap-rak-ka u₄-mu ṭup-⸢pi šu⸣-a-tu₂ ta-⸢mu⸣-[ru]

(o 3') [...] I am writing to you (again). The day you see this tablet of mine, [...] go to the land of Eriš and take a team of horses with you!

o 4'4'

[...] ⸢a-na⸣ kure-riš a-lik-ma KI-ka il₄-lat ANŠE.KUR.RA-MEŠe šu-pur-⸢ma⸣

o 5'5'

[...] ⸢tak-ri⸣-ṣi ša₂ ki-ma UR.⸢MAḪ da⸣-an ti-bu-šu₂-⸢un⸣

(o 5') [n] gnawing away [...] whose attack is as strong as a lion's;

o 6'6'

[...] ⸢lim⸣ ANŠE.KUR.RA-MEŠ BABBAR-⸢MEŠ⸣ ša₂ ⸢mi-kir⸣-šu₂-nu ṣal-mu

(o 6') [n] thousand white horses whose girth-straps are black;

o 7'7'

⸢1⸣.10 lim ANŠE.KUR.⸢RA⸣-MEŠ GE₆-MEŠ ⸢ša₂ mi-kir⸣-šu₂-nu pe-ṣu-u

(o 7') 70 thousand black horses whose girth-straps are white;

o 8'8'

1 ⸢me lim munusANŠE⸣.KUR.RA-MEŠ ša₂ KUŠ-ši-na pa-⸢an⸣ ka-⸢nak⸣-ti KURi šak-nu

(o 8') 1 hundred thousand mares whose skin is formed (like) the surface of a mountainous incense-bearing kanaktu-tree;

o 9'9'

40 lim AMAR-⸢MEŠ⸣ TUR ša₂ ⸢da⸣-ka-⸢ka⸣ la i-kil-lu-u

(o 9') 40 thousand young calves whose gamboling doesn't stop;

o 10'10'

50 lim ⸢ṣi⸣-in-da-at pa-⸢re⸣-e bit-ru-mu-ti

(o 10') 50 thousand teams of piebald mules;

o 11'11'

50 ⸢lim AMAR⸣-MEŠ tak-di-⸢ri⸣ ša₂ ṣu-pu-ru bu-⸢un⸣-nu-u₂ qar-nu šal-mu

(o 11') 50 thousand spirited calves whose hooves are beautified, whose horns are intact;

o 12'12'

20 lim dugDAL ⸢KAŠ?⸣ 1.30 lim DUG I₃.NUN.⸢NA⸣ 1.20 ⸢lim⸣ dugku-ut-tu gišGEŠTIN-MEŠ

(o 12') 20 thousand containers of beer, 90 thousand pots of butter, 80 thousand jugs of wine;

o 13'13'

1.20 lim ⸢ṢU⸣ KUR an-daḫ-ši 1.30 lim ⸢SAG.DU BANŠUR⸣-MEŠ⸢e⸣ GAL-MEŠ ša₂ ṣu-lum gišta-ti-di

(o 13') 80 thousand ... of andahšu-plants, 90 thousand great tabletops of the black wood of a tatīdu-tree;

o 14'14'

1 me lim ANŠE.NITA₂-MEŠe ⸢kar?-šu⸣ šimLI mul-la-⸢ma⸣ ana IGI-ka te-ram-ma al-ka

(o 14') Load (lit. fill) 1 hundred thousand donkeys with ... (and) burāšu-juniper, gather (all this) before you and come.

o 15'15'

1 ⸢na₄ši-bir⸣-ti KU₃.⸢SI₂₂ ḫu-uš?-šu₂⸣ 30 MA.NA lu KI.⸢LA₂⸣-BI ana ⸢i-rat?⸣ den-ki-du₃ ib-ri-ia lu-⸢kin?⸣  ; [...] lim tak₃-⸢kas₃⸣ na₄aš-pu-u na₄⸢ZA⸣.GIN₃ ⸢NA₄-MEŠ⸣ KUR ma-la ⸢GAL₂⸣u tak-ṣi-⸢ri⸣ [ina] ⸢muḫ⸣-ḫi lu-ban-ni!(NIN)

(o 15') I want to fix 1 lump of red gold - may its weight be 30 minas - to the chest of Enkidu, my friend; on (it) I want to fashion a string of amulet stones (with) [n] thousand small beads of chalcedony, lapis lazuli (and) mountain-stones, as many as there are.

o 16'16'

40 lim ⸢za?⸣-ḫa-⸢lu?⸣ AN.NA BABBARe ⸢a-na ERIM₄ EN GAL⸣[e d]⸢AMAR⸣.[UTU]

(o 16') 40 thousand silver alloy (and) white tin for the treasury of the great lord, Marduk.

o 17'17'

[...] ⸢GUN⸣ AN.BAR za-ka-a dam-qu ⸢na⸣-aš₂-[qu ...]

(o 17') [n] talents of iron: pure, fine, precious, [...],

Reverse
r 1r 1

[...]-⸢nu?⸣ la ra-šu-u ⸢KI?⸣-šu ⸢lu₂⸣[SIMUG? ...]

(r 1) [...], showing no [...] ... - with it the [smith ...].

r 22

[...] ⸢DU₁₀.GA za?-ku? ŠU₂? nam?-kur?⸣ in-neš-ru x [...]

(r 2) [...] good, pure, all of the property will be put in order ... [...].

r 33

[...] ⸢ša? ra?⸣-ma-ni-šu₂ ⸢mim-ma⸣ aq-ru ru-⸢u₂-qu⸣ ša₂ ul-tu ma-ti-⸢e IGI-MIN-MEŠ-a⸣-[a ...]  ; [... SUM]a u₃ ri-⸢qa⸣

(r 3) His own [...], something precious from afar, which my eyes [...] never [...], given or empty.

r 44

[...] ⸢ša?⸣ x-⸢ba?-li? a⸣-mur-ma a-na a-ḫa-meš qur-⸢rib⸣

(r 4) Select [...] of ... and bring (them) together!

r 55

[gišMA₂].⸢GUR₈?-MEŠ e⸣-ša₂-⸢a-ti? KU₃⸣.BABBAR KU₃.SI₂₂ mul-⸢la-am⸣-ma it-ti KU₃.BABBARim-ma KU₃.⸢SI₂₂⸣ a-na id₂BURANUN!(UD.NUN)[ki ...]

(r 5) Fill new boats with silver (and) gold, and [...] with the silver and gold to/on the Euphrates!

r 66

[a-na ka]-ar ⸢KA₂⸣.DINGIR.RAki ta-⸢bal?⸣-ma DINGIR-MEŠ-[a]-⸢a li⸣-mu-ra-ma lib₃-bi li-iḫ-mu

(r 6) Carry (them) off [to] the quay of Babylon so that my gods may see it and my heart may be stunned.

r 77

⸢šum?⸣-[ma] ina itiDUL U₄ ⸢15⸣-kam ina ⸢KA₂⸣.GAL IRI-ia ⸢ŠEŠ⸣.UNUGki la at-ma-ru-ka

(r 7) I will see you on the 15th day of Tašritu (month VII) at the city gate of my city Ur.

r 88

⸢at-ma⸣ DINGIR-MEŠ GAL-⸢MEŠ⸣ ša₂ ta-⸢mit⸣-su-nu la up-<ta>-sa-as-su

(r 8) I swear by the great gods whose oath cannot be annulled,

r 99

⸢at⸣-ma DINGIR-u₂-a dLUGAL.⸢BAN₃.DA⸣ d30 d⸢UTU⸣ dIGI.DU dLUGAL.IR₉.RA dMES.LAM.TA.E₃.A

(r 9) I swear by my gods Lugalbanda, Sin, Šamaš, Palil, Lugalira, Meslamtaea, Zababa and (my personal?) god, that I will send to you the ready lord of my officials, whose name you continually praise, so that he will wipe out your cities, pillage your palaces, ... [...] your gardens, fix lattices [in] the mouth of your rivers, (and) in the [...] of your fortified cities, who ... [...] I enter his dreams, so that [Gilgameš] will sit therein. Whoever [...]

r 1010

dza-ba₄-ba₄ u ⸢DINGIR⸣ be-li₂ SAG⸢ši?⸣-ia ZIbu ša₂ MU-šu tal-ta!(TU-)⸢nam₃⸣-[ma-ru?]

r 1111

a-šap-pa-rak-⸢kam₂-ma⸣ IRI-MEŠ-ka i-mar-raq [E₂].⸢GAL⸣-MEŠ-ka i-maš-ša₂-aʾ ⸢giš⸣KIRI₆-MEŠ-ka x [...]

r 1212

[ina] ⸢KA⸣ ID₂-MEŠ-⸢ka mur⸣-de-e u₂-rat-ta ⸢ina?⸣ [...]-⸢MEŠ⸣ IRI dan-nu-ti-ka ša x [...]

r 1313
